task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Support Triage (AI-assisted)** (Level 9)

Build a support ticket triage system. Tickets have subject, body, from_email, category, priority, status. Use a Claude agent named 'Triage' that receives a ticket and returns a JSON object with {category, priority}. When a new ticket is POSTed, call the Triage agent to classify it before saving. Need: POST /api/tickets (auto-classifies), GET /api/tickets (list), GET /api/tickets/open.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for web
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

create a Tickets table:
  subject, required
  body
  from_email
  category, default 'General'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  status, default 'open'

owner is 'support@example.com'
allow signup and login

agent 'Triage' receives ticket:
  response = ask claude 'You are a support ticket triage expert. Classify this ticket into a category (Bug, Feature Request, Account, Billing, General) and assign a priority (High, Normal, Low) based on the subject and body.' with ticket returning JSON text:
    category
    priority
  send back response

when user calls POST /api/tickets sending ticket:
  validate ticket:
    subject is text, required
  triage_result = call 'Triage' with ticket
  set ticket's category to triage_result's category
  set ticket's priority to triage_result's priority
  saved = save ticket to Tickets
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/tickets:
  tickets = get all Tickets
  send back tickets

when user calls GET /api/tickets/open:
  tickets = get all Tickets where status is 'open'
  send back tickets

page 'Support Inbox' at '/':
  on page load get open_tickets from '/api/tickets/open'
  heading 'Support Inbox'
  section 'New Ticket':
    'Subject' as text input saved as subject
    'Body' as text area saved as body
    'From Email' as text input saved as from_email
    button 'Submit':
      send {subject: subject, body: body, from_email: from_email} as a new ticket to '/api/tickets'
  section 'Open Tickets':
    display open_tickets as table
